Enabled.Name: Enable Skjaldborg full combat workflow
Enabled.Hint: Adds Planning, Movement, Resolution, and Bookkeeping workflow automation to AoV combats.

RequireAllCommit.Name: Require all capable combatants to commit
RequireAllCommit.Hint: Optional strict mode. When enabled, Planning cannot advance until every capable combatant has committed or held an intent. Disabled by default so the GM may advance freely.

MovementRounding.Name: Movement DEX penalty rounding
MovementRounding.Hint: How partial 3 m / 10 ft movement units are converted into DEX penalties.

MovementTickDelay.Name: Movement tick delay
MovementTickDelay.Hint: Delay in milliseconds between Skjaldborg movement checkpoints during the Foundry v14 movement phase.

ChatReports.Name: Post phase reports to chat
ChatReports.Hint: Creates short chat cards when phases advance and resolution queues are generated.

Debug.Name: Debug logging
Debug.Hint: Enable full Skjaldborg diagnostics with one switch, including general logs and all former advanced movement diagnostics at trace detail.

PerformanceDiagnostics.Name: Performance diagnostics
PerformanceDiagnostics.Hint: Collect client-side Skjaldborg render, cache, socket, and movement timing counters. Disabled by default.

MovementDebugMenu.ExportCopied: Movement debug JSON copied to clipboard and logged to the console.
MovementDebugMenu.ExportLogged: Movement debug JSON logged to the console.

ActionUiMenu.Name: Action interface configuration
ActionUiMenu.Label: Configure action interface
ActionUiMenu.Hint: Configure the shared quick-access circles, token action ring, and selected-actor hotbar in one client-specific submenu.
ActionUiMenu.Title: Skjaldborg Action Interface

ActionRingGroup.Name: Token action ring
ActionRingGroup.Hint: Control the radial action selector opened from the core Token HUD and the quick-access circles shared with the actor hotbar.

ActorHotbarGroup.Name: Selected-actor hotbar
ActorHotbarGroup.Hint: Control the actor-specific replacement hotbar shown for one unambiguous owned actor.

ReportMenu.Name: Phase report configuration
ReportMenu.Label: Configure phase reports
ReportMenu.Hint: Choose which phases create reports and control chat visibility, recipients, and combatant detail scope.
ReportMenu.Title: Skjaldborg Phase Reports

Phases.Name: Report phases
Phases.Hint: Post a report when entering each selected phase. Multiple phases may be selected.

Delivery.Name: Delivery mode
Delivery.Hint: Public cards create one all-combatant message visible to everyone. Whispered delivery creates only the audience-specific private cards.

Recipients.Name: Whisper recipients
Recipients.Hint: Used only for whispered delivery. Gamemasters receive one full report. Including players creates a separate scoped player report that is not shown in the gamemaster chat log.

Scope.Name: Player report scope
Scope.Hint: Applies only to the player whisper when recipients include players. GM reports always include all capable combatants. Public reports include all combatants. GM-private intent text is never included.

ActionRing.Name: Enable token action ring
ActionRing.Hint: Adds a radial action selector to the core Token HUD. It mirrors the represented actor's configured quick-access circles, including during Skjaldborg combat.

ActionRingMaxItems.Name: Quick-access circles
ActionRingMaxItems.Hint: Sets 6 to 12 visible quick-access circles for the actor hotbar and token action ring.

ActorHotbar.Name: Enable actor hotbar
ActorHotbar.Hint: Shows a selected-actor hotbar with resources, customizable quick access, Skjaldborg intent controls, sheet-derived statistics, history and family, skills, magic, equipment, effects, and macros.

ReplaceCoreHotbar.Name: Replace the core macro hotbar
ReplaceCoreHotbar.Hint: Hide the core macro hotbar while the custom actor hotbar has an owned actor. Macros remain available on the Macros tab.

ActorHotbarScale.Name: Actor hotbar scale
ActorHotbarScale.Hint: Scales the complete actor hotbar for this client.

ActorHotbarActionWidth.Name: Actor action-bar maximum width
ActorHotbarActionWidth.Hint: Sets the maximum width of the named actor-action lists and user macro grid.

ActorHotbarPosition.Name: Actor hotbar position
ActorHotbarPosition.Label: Reset position
ActorHotbarPosition.Hint: Return the draggable actor hotbar to its default position above the core hotbar.

ActorHotbarOpacity.Name: Actor hotbar resting opacity
ActorHotbarOpacity.Hint: Sets the hotbar opacity while it is idle. Hovering, focusing an input, or dragging temporarily restores full opacity.

ActionUiTheme.Name: Action interface visual theme
ActionUiTheme.Hint: Choose the Age of Vikings textured presentation or the classic presentation for the token action ring and actor hotbar.

PhaseStructureMenu.Name: Combat phase structure
PhaseStructureMenu.Label: Configure combat phases
PhaseStructureMenu.Hint: Choose which Skjaldborg phases appear in each logical combat round, or apply the optional Streamlined Combat structure.
PhaseStructureMenu.Title: Skjaldborg Combat Phase Structure
PhaseStructureMenu.Intro: The selected phases are shown in canonical order. Disabled phases are skipped visibly, while required movement, resolution preparation, bookkeeping, and round-reset automation is preserved at the appropriate boundary.

Phases.Name: Phases present in each round
Phases.Hint: At least one phase must remain enabled. The default tactical structure uses all four phases.

intent.Hint: Participants declare and commit actions and planned movement before acting.

movement.Hint: All predeclared nonengaged movement routes execute together before resolution.

resolution.Hint: Actions, missiles, spells, reactions, and the DEX-ordered queue are resolved.

bookkeeping.Hint: Round outcomes are finalized and prepared for application before the next round.

PhaseStructureMenu.StreamlinedHint: The streamlined preset keeps Resolution as the visible DEX countdown. Declarations and movement are handled immediately when announced, while bookkeeping is finalized immediately after each resolved outcome.

CombatTrackingMenu.Name: Combat tracking
CombatTrackingMenu.Label: Configure combat tracking
CombatTrackingMenu.Hint: Configure Planning initiative behavior, phase validation, movement DEX rounding, route preview visibility, and RAW automation references in one GM-only submenu.
CombatTrackingMenu.Title: Skjaldborg Combat Tracking
CombatTrackingMenu.Intro: These world settings control Planning turn behavior, declaration validation, and how movement contributes to DEX and engagement. Phase navigation remains GM-authoritative.

Navigation.Name: Phase navigation
Navigation.Hint: Choose sequential or simultaneous live initiative during Planning. The all-intents requirement remains optional; when disabled, the GM may advance even if some combatants have not declared an action.

Movement.Name: Movement and DEX
Movement.Hint: Configure movement penalty rounding, simultaneous movement timing, and planned route preview visibility.

Automation.Name: Automation references
Automation.Hint: Configure external documents used by Skjaldborg combat automation without hard-coding a single compendium source.

ReachVisualizerMenu.Name: Reach visualizer
ReachVisualizerMenu.Label: Configure reach visualizer
ReachVisualizerMenu.Hint: Configure the client-side canvas overlay for readied-weapon engagement reach.
ReachVisualizerMenu.Title: Skjaldborg Reach Visualizer
ReachVisualizerMenu.Intro: These client settings control the canvas overlay that shows effective reach from each token's currently readied weapon.

DynamicPlanningInitiative.Name: Use simultaneous live initiative during Planning
DynamicPlanningInitiative.Hint: Optional advanced mode. Planning has no active combatant turn: initiative changes are tracked and re-sorted live, while Next Turn and Next Round advance to the next configured phase. Disable this to retain the normal sequential turn cursor during Planning.

MovementPlanVisibility.Name: Movement plan preview visibility
MovementPlanVisibility.Hint: Controls who can see planned movement trajectories by hovering over tokens before the Movement phase resolves.
MovementPlanVisibility.Permission: Permission based: GM, Owner, or Observer only

KnockbackFumbleTable.Name: Knockback Fumbles table
KnockbackFumbleTable.Hint: Optional RollTable reference used by failed knockback fumble prompts. Paste a RollTable UUID, world table ID/name, Chaosium CID, or leave blank to use rt..fumbles / the bundled core UUID fallback.
KnockbackFumbleTable.DropHint: Drop a RollTable here to fill this reference automatically.

EvadeFightingDefensively.Name: Treat Evade as Fighting Defensively
EvadeFightingDefensively.Hint: When enabled, Evade is tagged for the universal Fighting Defensively option: the combatant forfeits attacks/actions for the round and the first substituted Dodge does not increase the cumulative Dodge/Parry penalty. When disabled, Evade is tagged only for AoV ranged moving-target penalties.

Enabled.Name: Enable reach overlay
Enabled.Hint: Show effective readied-weapon engagement reach on the canvas.

Visibility.Name: Visibility
Visibility.Hint: Choose whether reach appears for hovered tokens, all visible tokens, or all visible tokens with active/passive opacity.

Shape.Name: Shape
Shape.Hint: Use a square-grid outline where supported, or a circular fallback.

Opacity.Name: Opacity
Opacity.Hint: Overlay opacity used outside dynamic visibility mode.

PassiveOpacity.Name: Passive opacity
PassiveOpacity.Hint: Opacity for non-active tokens in dynamic visibility mode.

ActiveOpacity.Name: Active opacity
ActiveOpacity.Hint: Opacity for hovered or controlled tokens in dynamic visibility mode.

Hud.Title: Skjaldborg
Hud.MovementNote: Drag a combatant token during Planning to declare its route. The token stays in place until the Movement phase.
Hud.NoLedger: DEX is calculated when the GM advances from Movement to Resolution.
Hud.NoQueue: No resolution queue has been built yet.
Hud.MovementNoteImmediate: Drag the combatant token to announce movement. The route is captured and executed immediately after the GM persists it.
Hud.NoLedgerImmediate: DEX is calculated immediately when the action is announced and refreshed after immediate movement completes.

Dex.CarryoverDexZero: DEX rank was reduced to 0 or below.

Chat.NoCombatants: No combatants match this report scope.
Chat.FullMovementRule: Full movement only sets final DEX to 0.
Chat.PreventedRule: Final DEX is 0 or below; the action carries over.

RunicMagic.Title: Runic Magic
RunicMagic.ReadWriteRunesMissing: Read/Write (runes) is not present on this actor.
RunicMagic.NoMagic: This actor has no Rune Script or Seidur items.
RunicMagic.Unopposed: No selected targets: unopposed Rune Magic test.
RunicMagic.StateCarving: Carving is in progress. If not disrupted, this script can be sung in logical round {round}.
RunicMagic.StateFailed: The Craft attempt failed; a new carving attempt is required next round.
RunicMagic.StateDisrupted: The carving was disrupted by damage and must restart next round.
RunicMagic.SeidurRule: Seidur is tracked as a long ritual: 10 minutes per MP invested. It does not use the Rune Script two-round combat timing.
RunicMagic.ChatCarving: {actor} carves and dyes {script}. Rune count {runes}; the galdur is timed at DEX rank penalty {dex}.
RunicMagic.ChatCustomCraft: Craft is being resolved manually at {target}%.
RunicMagic.ChatCarvingRule: If the runemaster is wounded while carving and dyeing the runes, the delicate work is disrupted and a new attempt is required next melee round.
RunicMagic.ChatCast: {actor} sings the galdur for {script}. DEX rank penalty {dex}.
RunicMagic.ChatDisrupted: {actor}'s carving of {script} is disrupted by damage.
RunicMagic.ChatDisruptedRule: The casting procedure resets; a new attempt must be made in the next melee round.
RunicMagic.ChatSeidur: {actor} tracks the Seidur ritual {spell}. Realm {realm}; {mp} MP; {locked} locked MP; {time} minutes.
RunicMagic.TargetUnavailable: The target {target} is unavailable for Runic Magic resistance.

Results.Critical: The magical rune script works with double effectiveness, producing an effect twice as potent. If it inflicts damage, that damage is doubled. If the script has an intended duration, the runemaster may choose to extend it. The required magic points are expended.
Results.Special: The magical rune script works with 1.5 x effectiveness. The required magic points are expended.
Results.Success: The magical rune script works just as the runemaster intended, with the predicted intensity. The required magic points are expended.
Results.Failure: A mistake was made in the singing of the galdur, and the magical effect fails to manifest. The required magic points are still locked in the carved runes of the script, and the runemaster may make another attempt to sing the galdur and unleash its power in the next melee round with a new Rune Magic roll.
Results.Fumble: The magical effect manifests, but it is spectacularly twisted. One of the runes of the script, chosen by the gamemaster, has its meaning and Use/Effect reversed, with disastrous repercussions. The required magic points are expended.

SeidurResults.Critical: The practitioner can touch the threads of fate with great precision and may choose to increase any one spell element by up to three additional levels, to a maximum level of 6.
SeidurResults.Special: The spell works even better than expected. The practitioner may choose to increase Dimension by one additional level, to a maximum level of 6.
SeidurResults.Failure: The spell fails to produce any magical effect. The practitioner may attempt the same ritual again on the next day.
SeidurResults.Fumble: The practitioner loses control of the magical forces, and they act in some way opposite to how it was intended.

MovementAutomation.EngagedCannotMove: This combatant is engaged and cannot declare ordinary movement.

Diagnostics.Failed: Skjaldborg diagnostics found {count} issue(s). See the console table for details.

Warnings.GmOnly: Only a GM can perform this combat control.
Warnings.NoGm: No active GM is available to receive the combat request.
Warnings.GmChanged: The responding GM changed before this combat request was acknowledged. Try the action again.
Warnings.SocketTimeout: The active GM did not acknowledge the combat request.
Warnings.NoCombat: No active combat is available for this control.
Warnings.NotOwner: You do not control this combatant.
Warnings.StaleDocument: This combat state changed after the HUD rendered. Reopen or refresh the HUD and try again.
Warnings.InvalidPhase: That phase transition is not legal from the current phase.
Warnings.MovementRunning: Movement automation is still resolving. Wait for movement to complete before advancing.
Warnings.MovementPlansPending: Cannot begin Movement: the plans for {names} are still synchronizing or unfinished. Wait for the movement-plan confirmation, or clear and plan those routes again.
Warnings.MovementRouteTruncated: A movement route exceeded {limit} points and was truncated. Enable Debug logging for full movement diagnostics.
Warnings.NoCombatant: Select or activate a combatant first.
Warnings.Uncommitted: Cannot advance: these combatants have not committed or held intent: {names}
Warnings.FormUnavailable: The Skjaldborg combat HUD form is unavailable.
Warnings.ItemUnavailable: That actor item is no longer available.
Warnings.ActionFailed: The selected action could not be completed. See the browser console for details.
Warnings.ResourceUpdateFailed: The actor resource could not be updated. See the browser console for details.
Warnings.StatUnavailable: That actor statistic is no longer available.
Warnings.WeaponMustBeCarried: Only a carried weapon can be drawn.
Warnings.SelectCarriedWeapon: Select a carried weapon to draw.
Warnings.AtLeastOnePhase: At least one combat phase must remain enabled. Resolution has been retained as the safety fallback.
Warnings.DisabledPhase: That phase is disabled in the current combat phase structure.
Warnings.MovementDraftsSkipped: The following combatants still have unfinished movement drafts; those drafts will not execute: {names}
Warnings.WellbeingUpdateFailed: The actor wellbeing data could not be updated. See the browser console for details.
Warnings.SelectWeapon: Select a carried weapon to roll.
Warnings.MagicPreparationFailed: The magic preparation state could not be updated. See the browser console for details.
Warnings.AttackTargetRequired: Target at least one Token before opening an attack roll.
Warnings.SingleAttackTargetRequired: The attack workflow requires one resolving target at a time.
Warnings.AttackTargetUnavailable: The targeted Token does not represent an available actor.
Warnings.NoAttackWeapons: {actor} has no attack-ready weapon. Ready a weapon, or add a Hand-to-hand or natural weapon, before opening an attack roll.
Warnings.CannotRetreatFromMounted: A fighter cannot normally disengage from a mounted opponent unless they can hide, interpose an obstacle, or are defended by another combatant. Use Flee or ask the GM for an override.
Warnings.RetreatDefensiveOnly: This combatant is making a fighting retreat and can only declare defensive actions until the retreat resolves.
Warnings.MissileTargetRequired: Target at least one Token before opening a missile roll.
Warnings.SingleMissileTargetRequired: The missile workflow requires one resolving target at a time.
Warnings.MissileTargetUnavailable: The targeted Token does not represent an available actor.
Warnings.NoMissileWeapons: {actor} has no readied missile or thrown weapon. Ready a missile or thrown weapon before opening a missile roll.
Warnings.NoReadiedMissileWeapon: The actor no longer has a readied missile or thrown weapon.
Warnings.NoMissileAmmunition: {actor} has no available ammunition for {weapon}. Add carried gear named arrow, bolt, or round before firing.
Warnings.AmmunitionRequired: Select available ammunition before firing this weapon.
Warnings.OpenCombatCard: Resolve or close the existing AoV combat chat card before starting another attack workflow.
Warnings.NoDefenderDefense: {actor} has no weapon or Dodge skill available for the defender roll.
Warnings.AmmunitionDeductFailed: The missile action could not be completed or ammunition could not be deducted. See the browser console for details.
Warnings.KnockbackTargetRequired: Select at least one target before attempting a knockback.
Warnings.SingleKnockbackTargetRequired: The knockback workflow requires one resolving target at a time.
Warnings.NoKnockbackWeapons: {actor} has no readied weapon, shield, natural weapon, or hand-to-hand attack available for a knockback.
Warnings.GrappleTargetRequired: Select at least one target before attempting a grapple.
Warnings.SingleGrappleTargetRequired: The grapple workflow requires one resolving target at a time.
Warnings.SocketUserMissing: The target user for this combat prompt is unavailable.
Warnings.SocketUserInactive: {user} is not active and cannot receive this combat prompt.
Warnings.SocketUserTimeout: {user} did not acknowledge the combat prompt.
Warnings.NoDefensePromptUser: No active owner or GM is available to receive the defender roll dialog.
Warnings.NoDefensePromptRoute: The defender roll belongs on another user's client, but no routing callback was available.
Warnings.DefenseActorUnavailable: The defender actor is unavailable on this client.
Warnings.DefenseActorNotOwned: You do not control the defender actor.
Warnings.DefenseCombatCardUnavailable: The AoV combat chat card was not available on this client in time for the defender roll.
Warnings.DefensePromptCancelled: No defense was added to the opposed combat card.
Warnings.EvadeEffectUnavailable: The Evading status effect could not be applied to this actor.

ActionRing.IntentHint: Click to commit. Shift-click or right-click for details.
ActionRing.ItemHint: Click to use. Right-click opens Item details when available.
ActionRing.QuickAccessHint: Click to use a quick-access action. Right-click opens Item details when available.

ActorHotbar.IntentControlHint: Left click - interact. Right click - set intent.
ActorHotbar.NoMagic: No magic or powers are available.
ActorHotbar.Move: Right-drag portrait to move actor hotbar
ActorHotbar.NoStats: No rollable actor statistics are available.
ActorHotbar.NoEquipment: No weapons, gear, or armour are available.

QuickAccess.RemoveHint: Right-click to remove from quick access.
QuickAccess.PortraitDropHint: Drop an actor-hotbar action on the portrait to assign it to the first empty quick-access slot.
QuickAccess.Full: All visible quick-access circles are occupied. Drop directly onto a circle to replace or swap it, or increase the circle count in settings.

ActorHotbar.NoHistoryFamily: No history, family, passion, devotion, thrall, or farm records are available.
ActorHotbar.NoHitLocations: No hit locations are available for this actor.
ActorHotbar.DockMove: Click to collapse or expand; drag around the portrait to dock action panels left, right, above, or below.
ActorHotbar.ReactionControlHint: Reactions: left-click to add one; right-click to remove one.

Tracker.SimultaneousGroup: Member of a simultaneous resolution group
Tracker.CantEngage: Can't Engage: moved {gridUnits} grid units; engagement limit {limit} grid units.

Delay.Title: Delay Action
Delay.RawHint: Delay to a later DEX rank, or wait for another combatant and resolve the ordering with INT.

Utility.Title: Utility
Utility.TwoWeaponAvailable: Two eligible readied weapons are available.
Utility.TwoWeaponUnavailable: Ready two non-natural weapons with 100% or higher skill.
Utility.ShieldLocationWarning: RAW shield cover normally protects exactly three hit locations.
Utility.ShieldwallStateHint: Declare shieldwall state for manual adjudication.

DamageEffects.ImpaledDescription: {weapon} is stuck in {target}. No healing of this injury until the weapon is removed. Attacker may withdraw it later with half weapon skill; target may pull it out with (STR+CON)x2. Moving while impaled causes half normal weapon damage per round. If armor stopped penetration, remove this effect.

InjuryDescriptions.Leg1x: Leg can't be used. Apply Prone. No further action this round. Prone attack penalties apply.
InjuryDescriptions.Arm1x: Arm can't be used. Drop unsecured held items. The character may continue with other usable limbs.
InjuryDescriptions.Abdomen1x: Both legs can't be used. Apply Prone. Bleed out to death within 10 minutes without First Aid or healing.
InjuryDescriptions.Chest1x: Apply Prone. Cannot act. Bleed out to death within 10 minutes without First Aid.
InjuryDescriptions.Head1x: Unconscious and Prone. Death within 5 minutes unless First Aid or healing restores at least 1 HP.
InjuryDescriptions.Location1x: Location can't be used. Apply the appropriate RAW location consequence.
InjuryDescriptions.Limb2x: Damage from this single blow is capped at 2x location HP for total HP loss. Functionally incapacitated and in shock until healed.
InjuryDescriptions.Vital2x: Unconscious and Prone. Lose 1 HP per combat round until First Aid or healing is applied.
InjuryDescriptions.Limb3x: Limb is severed or irrevocably maimed. Functionally incapacitated. Severed limbs require extraordinary regrowth; maiming must be fully healed within 10 minutes or the limb remains useless.

Weapons.DrawHint: Ready the selected carried weapon and reduce initiative by 5 DEX.
Weapons.SheatheHint: Sheathe the currently readied weapon and reduce initiative by 5 DEX.
Weapons.DropHint: Clear the currently readied weapon without changing initiative.
Weapons.RollSelectedHint: Roll an attack with the selected carried weapon.
Weapons.RollAttackHint: Roll an attack with this weapon.
Weapons.RollDamageHint: Roll this weapon's damage using the Age of Vikings damage workflow.

IntentDialog.Title: Other Intent
IntentDialog.Label: Describe the intended action
IntentDialog.Placeholder: Enter the action or declaration that other players should see…

AttackDialog.Title: Attack Roll
AttackDialog.TwoWeaponHalfSkillTooltip: Use the RAW two-weapon half-skill subdivision for this attack.
AttackDialog.AimedNoLocations: The target has no selectable hit location or equipped weapon.
AttackDialog.AimedLocationUnavailable: The aimed hit location is no longer available; using the normal hit-location workflow.
AttackDialog.AimedEquipmentUnavailable: The aimed weapon or shield is no longer available; using the normal hit-location workflow.
AttackDialog.AimedEquipmentDamageCard: Aimed: {weapon} takes {weaponDamage} damage from {damage} rolled damage.
AttackDialog.DisarmNoWeapons: The target has no equipped weapon or shield to disarm.
AttackDialog.StunNoHead: The target has no selectable hit location.
AttackDialog.StunHeadUnavailable: The selected Stun hit location is no longer available.
AttackDialog.Summary: Base {base}% · Situation {situation}% · Aimed {aimed}% · Disarm {disarm}% · Stun {stun}% · Augment {augment}%
AttackDialog.ScaffoldHint: This workflow stage sends the attack and target defense into the core Age of Vikings opposed combat card.
AttackDialog.DamageTypeSwitchTooltip: {label} ({abbreviation}). Click to switch to {nextLabel} ({nextAbbreviation}). Weapon type: {sourceLabel} ({sourceAbbreviation}).
AttackDialog.MeleeMissileDamageTooltip: Melee use of {sourceLabel} ({sourceAbbreviation}) is treated as {label} ({abbreviation}). Use the Missile action for the weapon's ranged damage type.

StunDialog.NoStunDamage: The blow does not penetrate head armor; no stun resistance roll is needed.
StunDialog.UnsupportedDamageFormula: Skjaldborg could not calculate minimum Stun damage from this weapon formula.
StunDialog.MissingActors: The Stun attacker or target is no longer available.
StunDialog.MissingStatusTarget: The stunned actor or head location is no longer available.
StunDialog.RecoveryPrompt: {target} may roll CON x1 to recover consciousness.
StunDialog.RecoverySuccess: {target} recovers consciousness and takes 1 point of head damage.
StunDialog.RecoveryFailure: {target} remains stunned and takes 1 point of head damage.
StunDialog.RecoveryAlreadyResolved: This Stun recovery prompt has already been resolved.

DisarmDialog.TargetWeaponUnavailable: The selected Disarm target weapon is no longer available.
DisarmDialog.StrikeWeaponDamageCard: Disarm: {weapon} takes {weaponDamage} damage from {damage} rolled damage.
DisarmDialog.StrikeWeaponNoDamage: {weapon} takes no damage; rolled damage {damage} did not exceed its current hit points.
DisarmDialog.FlatDamageCard: Disarm: hit with the flat for {damage} damage.
DisarmDialog.FlatSuccessDistance: {weapon} is knocked from the target's hand and flies {distance} meter(s).
DisarmDialog.FlatSuccessFeet: {weapon} is knocked from the target's hand and lands at their feet.
DisarmDialog.FlatFailed: {weapon} is struck, but remains in hand.
DisarmDialog.EntangleRequiresSpecial: Entangle requires a special or critical attack success; no disarm occurs.
DisarmDialog.EntangleSuccess: {weapon} is wrenched from the defender's hand.
DisarmDialog.EntangleCounterSuccess: The defender takes {weapon} from the attacker.
DisarmDialog.EntangleCounterFailed: The defender fails to take the entangling weapon; no disarm occurs.

TargetQueue.Title: {workflow} target queue
TargetQueue.Body: Resolve each remaining target when the current AoV combat card is closed.
TargetQueue.MissingDocuments: The queued target, actor, or item is no longer available.

SplitAttack.Title: Split Attack
SplitAttack.Body: {actor} splits the attack, second attack taking place at DEX rank {dex}.
SplitAttack.Hint: Split natural weapon skill into two attacks. Each value must be 50% or higher and the total cannot exceed natural skill.
SplitAttack.Unavailable: Requires natural weapon skill of 100% or higher. Temporary, magical, and dialog modifiers do not count.
SplitAttack.Invalid: Split attacks must both be at least 50%, and their combined chance cannot exceed natural weapon skill.

DisengageDialog.Title: Disengage
DisengageDialog.Intro: Choose how this combatant attempts to break close combat engagement.
DisengageDialog.OpportunityAttacker: Opponent making the immediate Flee attack
DisengageDialog.FleeOpportunityBody: {attacker} may make one immediate attack against {target}. This attack cannot be parried or dodged and does not consume the attacker's normal action.

retreat.Label: Retreat
retreat.Description: Spend this combat round doing nothing except Parry or Dodge. Engagement clears before the next Planning phase.

flee.Label: Flee
flee.Description: Run immediately. Selected engaged opponents get immediate attacks which cannot be parried or dodged.

knockback.Label: Knockback
knockback.Description: A successful knockback attack can clear engagement with the knocked-back opponent.

DisengageDialog.OpponentSelectionTooltip: Toggle whether {opponent} is included in this disengagement declaration.

MissileDialog.Title: Missile Roll
MissileDialog.AimedNoLocations: The target has no selectable hit location or equipped weapon.
MissileDialog.IntoMeleeRandomTarget: The missile attack missed the intended target; roll for random target.
MissileDialog.RangeProjectileOnly: Only projectile missile weapons can use medium or long range.
MissileDialog.Summary: Base {base}% · Situation {situation}% · Aimed {aimed}% · Range {range} · Into Melee {intoMelee} · Augment {augment}%
MissileDialog.ScaffoldHint: This workflow stage sends the missile attack and target defense into the core Age of Vikings opposed combat card. Required ammunition is deducted when the request is submitted.

KnockbackDialog.Title: Knockback Roll
KnockbackDialog.ScaffoldHint: This starts a core Age of Vikings combat attack card. When the attack resolves successfully, a RAW knockback resistance card is created.
KnockbackDialog.MissingResistanceActors: Unable to create the knockback resistance card because one of the actors is unavailable.
KnockbackDialog.AttackerFalls: {actor} fails to keep footing and falls prone.
KnockbackDialog.AttackerBouncesBack: {actor} bounces off the target and is knocked back {distance} grid(s).
KnockbackDialog.FumblePromptBody: Resolve with the configured Fumbles table.
KnockbackDialog.ResistancePublicSummary: STR+SIZ vs SIZ+DEX after successful attack
KnockbackDialog.RecoveryChoicePrompt: Choose whether the attacker falls prone or bounces back from the target.

GrappleDialog.Title: Grapple Roll
GrappleDialog.RawHint: RAW: a successful initial Grapple catches the hit location rolled; weapon parries catch the weapon arm; shield parries grasp the shield.
GrappleDialog.ResistancePublicSummary: Initial hold uses Grapple. Immobilize uses Grappler STR vs Target STR; Throw uses Grappler STR+DEX vs Target SIZ+DEX after a maintained hold.
GrappleDialog.ScaffoldHint: The Grapple workflow uses the AoV combat card first, then suppresses normal damage and resolves the RAW hold, immobilize, or throw branch after the card is closed.
GrappleDialog.WeaponArmChoicePrompt: The target parried with a weapon. Choose which hit location was caught.
GrappleDialog.NoActiveHold: No matching active Grappled hold was found.
GrappleDialog.InitialDodged: {target} dodges the initial grapple. No hold is established.
GrappleDialog.InitialBlocked: {target} blocks the hold with {defense}. No hold is established.
GrappleDialog.HoldBroken: {attacker} fails to maintain the hold on {target}; the grapple is broken.
GrappleDialog.ImmobilizeFailed: {attacker} keeps hold of {target}, but does not immobilize the grasped limb/object.
GrappleDialog.ThrowFailedHoldMaintained: {attacker} fails to throw {target}, but maintains the grapple.
GrappleDialog.ThrowDestinationPrompt: Choose where to throw the target: up to {distance} grid unit(s).
GrappleDialog.ThrowDestinationCanvasHint: Choose any highlighted square for the grapple throw, up to {distance} grid unit(s).
GrappleDialog.LandingSucceeded: {target} succeeds at DEX×5 ({result}, roll {roll}) and avoids landing damage.
GrappleDialog.LandingFailedDamageApplied: {target} fails DEX×5 ({result}, roll {roll}); landing roll {hitRoll} hits {location}. Damage {grossDamage} − armor {armor} = {netDamage}, applied.
GrappleDialog.LandingFailedDamagePosted: {target} fails DEX×5 ({result}, roll {roll}); landing roll {hitRoll} hits {location}. Damage {grossDamage} − armor {armor} = {netDamage}. Apply manually if needed.
